# Governance Directives

Follow these directives in every file you write or edit.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

These are invariants. Violation is never acceptable.

- Secrets — passwords, API keys, OAuth client secrets, JWT signing keys, TLS private keys, tokens — MUST NEVER appear as values in any tracked file. NEVER commit a real secret value to `application.yml`, profile-specific YAML, source code, fixtures, `.env.example`, configuration scripts, or any other file under version control. Inject secrets through environment variables resolved at runtime or through a dedicated secrets backend. (ref: INV-001)
- Files under `**/domain/**` MUST depend only on the Kotlin standard library, `java.time.*`, and other domain types. NEVER import from Spring (`org.springframework.*`), Spring Data (`org.springframework.data.*`), Restate (`dev.restate.*`), Jakarta Servlet (`jakarta.servlet.*`), Jackson (`com.fasterxml.jackson.*`), `kotlinx.serialization.*`, Hibernate, OkHttp / WebClient, or any messaging-framework package inside `domain/`. (ref: INV-002)
- Domain event history MUST be written and read exclusively via Axon Framework's APIs (`AggregateLifecycle.apply(...)`, `EventGateway`, `QueryGateway`, `EventStore`). NEVER persist domain events to any other store as the system of record, NEVER bypass these APIs to write directly to Axon's underlying storage, and NEVER read events from anywhere other than Axon's APIs. (ref: INV-003)
- Every Gradle invocation — local, CI, Dockerfile, IDE configuration, release pipeline — MUST go through the project's pinned wrapper at `./gradlew` (with `gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties` containing both a `distributionUrl` to a specific Gradle version and a non-empty `distributionSha256Sum`). NEVER invoke a system-installed `gradle` binary, NEVER ship a checkout missing `gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, or the wrapper properties, and NEVER weaken the version pin to `-latest` or remove the SHA-256 sum. (ref: INV-004)

## Routing Table

Before starting a task, scan this table. If your task matches any signals or scopes, read the referenced file.

| Signals | Scope | File |
|---|---|---|
| `.kt`/`.kts` files, Kotlin idioms, null safety, `data class`, sealed types, coroutines, `Dispatchers`, Spring constructor injection, Axon commands/events | implementation, review | `governance/kotlin.md` |
| `*.gradle.kts`, `settings.gradle.kts`, `gradle/libs.versions.toml`, dependency declarations, plugin application, version catalog, lock files, configuration cache, convention plugins, `buildSrc/` | implementation, review | `governance/gradle.md` |
| `@RestController`/`@Controller`, `@ConfigurationProperties`, `@Document`, `@Profile`, `application*.yml`, Spring Data Mongo, Bean Validation, `@RestControllerAdvice`, Actuator, `@SpringBootTest` / `@WebMvcTest` / `@DataMongoTest` | implementation, review | `governance/spring-boot.md` |
| domain layer, application layer, ports & adapters, aggregates, value objects, entities, `domain/**`, `application/**`, `adapter/in/**`, `adapter/out/**`, CQS, anemic domain model, hexagonal/DDD | design, implementation, review | `governance/hexagonal-ddd.md` |
| Gradle module organization, single-module vs multi-module, `settings.gradle.kts`, layer-first vs feature-first, screaming architecture, ArchUnit, Konsist, arch test, layer dependency rules, top-level package layout, `src/main/kotlin/<root>/` structure | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/architecture.md` |
| persistence, `EventStorageEngine` (Jpa/Jdbc/Mongo/InMemory), Spring Data starter (`spring-boot-starter-data-*`), MongoDB, `MongoTemplate`, JDBC driver, Postgres, Elasticsearch, Cassandra, Redis-as-store, two-store policy, persistence surface, cache-as-system-of-record, Mongock, `@ChangeUnit`, MongoDB migrations, `auto-index-creation`, Mongobee, Liquibase Mongo, IndexOps runner | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/persistence.md` |
| OAuth 2.0, OIDC, JWT validation, JWKS, Zitadel, identity provider, IdP, Spring Security `oauth2.resourceserver`, `JwtDecoder`, `JwtAuthenticationConverter`, `tid` claim, federation, Google sign-in, RS256 / ES256 / HS256, refresh-token rotation, access-token lifetime, token introspection | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/auth.md` |
| `@TargetAggregateIdentifier`, `@CommandHandler`, `@EventSourcingHandler`, `AggregateLifecycle.apply(...)`, Axon Server, Dynamic Consistency Boundary (DCB), domain events, projections, read models, upcasters, event store | design, implementation, review | `governance/event-sourcing.md` |
| Axon Framework 5 vs 4, DCB / Dynamic Consistency Boundary, Restate, `dev.restate.*`, `@Saga` / saga, `CommandGateway`, `QueryGateway`, durable execution, durable workflow, orchestration boundary, `adapter/in/restate/**`, axon-restate division of labor | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/orchestration.md` |
| multi-tenant, tenant isolation, `TenantId`, `tenantId`, JWT `tid` claim, `OncePerRequestFilter`, `TenantContext`, tenant-scoped query, `@Document` tenant field, single Axon Server context, row-level filter, command/event/query payload tenant | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/multi-tenancy.md` |
| OpenAPI 3.1 spec, Swagger UI, Zalando RESTful API Guidelines (foundation lock), RFC 7807 / `application/problem+json`, cursor pagination, `kebab-case` paths, `snake_case` query parameters, `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` enums, OAuth 2.0 Bearer auth, trailing-slash rejection, Google AIP / Microsoft REST / JSON:API rejection, `Deviates from MUST <NNN>` deviation registry | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/api-rules.md` |
| local-dev topology, `compose.yaml` / `docker-compose.yml`, Docker Compose, infrastructure containers (mongodb, axon-server, restate, zitadel, zitadel-postgres), pinned image tags, single-JVM Spring Boot process, `Makefile` (`make up` / `make down` / `make run`), `.env` / `.env.example`, `127.0.0.1` port binding, Compose secrets hygiene | planning, design, implementation, review | `governance/local-dev.md` |

## Reminders

Before acting, check the relevant constraint.

[edikt:reminders:start]: #
- Before committing → scan the diff for any value that looks like a credential (password, key, token); if in doubt, replace it with a `${VAR_NAME}` placeholder and inject at runtime (ref: INV-001)
- Before adding an import to a `domain/` file → confirm the package is `kotlin.*`, `java.time.*`, or another domain type — never a framework, persistence, transport, or serialization package (ref: INV-002)
- Before writing a domain event → emit via `AggregateLifecycle.apply(...)`; never `mongoTemplate.save(event)`, never write to a parallel collection, never short-circuit Axon's event-store APIs (ref: INV-003)
- Before invoking Gradle from a script, Dockerfile, or CI workflow → use `./gradlew`, never bare `gradle` (ref: INV-004)
- Before introducing a new cross-aggregate operation → choose DCB (consistency-required) or a Restate workflow (orchestration-required); never reach for a saga — the codebase has no saga lane (ref: ADR-001)
- Before defining a new command, event, query, or read-model `@Document` → add a non-null `tenantId: TenantId` property; tenancy is the project's hardest cross-cutting invariant (ref: ADR-002)
- Before designing an HTTP error response → use RFC 7807 `application/problem+json` with `type`, `title`, `status`; never JSON:API error envelopes, custom error wrappers, or plain-text error bodies (ref: ADR-006)
- Before changing a Mongo schema or index → create a new `@ChangeUnit` under `adapter/out/readmodel/migrations/` with a unique `id` and increasing `order`; never modify an executed change unit (ref: ADR-007)
- Before adding a new infrastructure service to the dev environment → add it to the root `compose.yaml` with a pinned tag and a `127.0.0.1:` port binding; never install on the host, never use `:latest`, never bind to `0.0.0.0` (ref: ADR-008)
- Before referencing a new `${VAR}` from `compose.yaml` → add the same variable to `.env.example` at the repo root with a placeholder value and a one-line comment; never let the compose file reference an undocumented variable (ref: ADR-008)
[edikt:reminders:end]: #

## Verification Checklist

Before finishing, verify each item. If any fails, fix before submitting.

- [ ] No real-looking secret values in any tracked file; pre-commit and CI secret scanners (`gitleaks` / `trufflehog` / `detect-secrets`) configured, running, and passing (ref: INV-001)
- [ ] Architecture test (ArchUnit / Konsist / Modulint) for domain framework-import isolation is configured and passing in CI (ref: INV-002)
- [ ] No event-store writes outside Axon Framework's runtime, no direct queries against event collections, no outbox/shadow event store as a system of record (ref: INV-003)
- [ ] All builds invoke `./gradlew`; `gradle-wrapper.properties` has `distributionUrl` pinned to a specific version and a non-empty `distributionSha256Sum`; wrapper files are tracked (ref: INV-004)
- [ ] Every command, domain event, and query type carries a non-null `tenantId: TenantId` field (ArchUnit / Konsist arch test) (ref: ADR-002)
- [ ] Every `@Document` class for tenant-scoped read models has a `tenantId` field, and every compound index on those collections leads with `tenantId` (ref: ADR-002)
- [ ] No controller, filter, or interceptor reads `tenantId` from a request body, query parameter, URL path, or `X-Tenant-Id` (or similar) HTTP header — JWT `tid` claim is the only sanctioned source (ref: ADR-002)
- [ ] An ArchUnit / Konsist architecture test exists in `src/test/kotlin/` and is wired into `./gradlew check`; the test is not `@Disabled` and is not gated by any system-property or environment guard (ref: ADR-003)
- [ ] Resolved `runtimeClasspath` (from `./gradlew dependencies --configuration runtimeClasspath`) contains `spring-boot-starter-data-mongodb` and no other `spring-boot-starter-data-*` persistence starter (ref: ADR-004)
- [ ] No JDBC driver, Elasticsearch client, Cassandra client, or other non-Mongo / non-Axon persistence library is present in the resolved `runtimeClasspath` (ref: ADR-004)
- [ ] All 4xx/5xx response definitions in the OpenAPI document use the `application/problem+json` content type and conform to RFC 7807 (`type`, `title`, `status` minimum); no JSON:API error envelopes (`{"errors": [...]}`) or custom error wrappers anywhere in the spec or in `@RestControllerAdvice` code (ref: ADR-006)
- [ ] Every `@ChangeUnit`-annotated class lives under `**/adapter/out/readmodel/migrations/**`; ArchUnit / Konsist test asserts no change units exist in `domain/`, `application/`, `adapter/in/`, or `infrastructure/` (ref: ADR-007)
- [ ] `compose.yaml` (or `docker-compose.yml`) exists at the repo root with exactly five infra containers (`mongodb`, `axon-server`, `restate`, `zitadel`, `zitadel-postgres`); every `image:` tag is pinned (no `:latest`, `:edge`, `:stable`) (ref: ADR-008)
- [ ] A `Makefile` exists at the repo root defining `up`, `down`, and `run` targets that invoke (respectively) `docker compose up -d`, `docker compose down`, and `./gradlew bootRun`; docs and CI scripts reference `make up` / `make down` / `make run`, never the underlying commands (ref: ADR-008)
- [ ] No secret values appear inline in `compose.yaml`; every secret reference is `${VAR_NAME}` and every `${VAR}` is documented in a tracked `.env.example` (ref: ADR-008)

## Reminder: Non-Negotiable Constraints

These constraints were listed above and are restated for emphasis.
Do not violate them under any circumstances.

- Secrets — passwords, API keys, OAuth client secrets, JWT signing keys, TLS private keys, tokens — MUST NEVER appear as values in any tracked file. (ref: INV-001)
- Files under `**/domain/**` MUST depend only on the Kotlin standard library, `java.time.*`, and other domain types — NEVER on Spring, Spring Data, Restate, Jakarta Servlet, Jackson, `kotlinx.serialization`, Hibernate, OkHttp / WebClient, or any messaging-framework package. (ref: INV-002)
- Domain event history MUST be written and read exclusively via Axon Framework's APIs — NEVER persist events to any other store as the system of record, NEVER bypass Axon's event-store APIs. (ref: INV-003)
- Every Gradle invocation MUST go through `./gradlew` with a pinned `distributionUrl` and `distributionSha256Sum` — NEVER a system-installed `gradle`. (ref: INV-004)
